behavior: Social, noisy ground forager that hops and pecks for seeds, grains, insects, and discarded food; often feeds in flocks and nests in cavities or niches in buildings and other structures.
field_marks: Chunky, full-breasted sparrow with a round head, short tail, and stout conical bill.
Male: gray crown, white cheeks, chestnut nape, and a distinct black throat-and-breast bib.
Female and immature: plainer buffy brown and gray, with a pale eyebrow and gray-brown underparts.
Back and wings strongly streaked or patterned in brown, black, and buff; often shows a pale wingbar.
Dark eye; pinkish-brown legs and a short, squared-to-slightly-notched tail.
habitat: Closely associated with human-built environments, including towns, suburbs, farms, parks, and buildings; generally absent from extensive undisturbed woodland, forest, and grassland.

sources.title: House Sparrow Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
